scope_note: Seed catalog for Philippines health coverage literacy workflows. This is a category-level extraction surface, not a complete plan database.
advice_boundary: Educational workflow data only. This catalog does not provide medical advice, insurance advice, legal advice, financial advice, claims decisions, coverage determinations, rankings, or purchase recommendations.

sources.title: PhilHealth Benefits
sources.notes: Official PhilHealth benefit categories and benefit mechanics page.

sources.title: Insurance Commission Circular Letter 2017-19 on HMO products and forms
sources.notes: Official HMO product definition, product contract, and benefit-category source.

sources.title: Maxicare Plans
sources.notes: Provider-published product surface for individual/family, MSME, corporate, prepaid, and emergency-oriented Maxicare products.

sources.title: MediCard Healthcare Packages
sources.notes: Provider-published package archive for MediCard individual, family, SME, corporate, and emergency products.

sources.title: MediCard H.E.R.O. Health Emergency Response On-demand
PhilCare Services
sources.notes: Provider-published service surface for PhilCare Health LUXE and related coverage categories.

sources.title: AIA Philippines Health Products
sources.notes: Provider-published health product category page for AIA Philippines.

sources.title: Sun Life Philippines Health Protection Products
sources.notes: Provider-published health protection product category page.

regulatory_patterns.educational_summary: The Insurance Commission circular frames HMO products as prepaid healthcare-service arrangements delivered through a selected provider network for a specified period, with possible benefit categories including inpatient, outpatient, emergency, annual physical exam, executive check-up, dental, maternity, and ancillary services.
regulatory_patterns.advice_boundary: Use as a regulator-published product taxonomy only. Do not infer that any specific HMO plan includes every listed category.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: PhilHealth describes inpatient benefits as benefits paid to accredited health facilities through All Case Rates, with the case rate deducted from the member's bill before discharge.
user_questions: Is the facility PhilHealth-accredited for the case?
What documents does the facility require before discharge?
What part of the bill remains outside the PhilHealth case rate?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain the public benefit category only. Do not decide eligibility, reimbursement, admissibility, or final bill responsibility.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: PhilHealth lists outpatient benefit categories such as day surgeries, radiotherapy, hemodialysis, and outpatient blood transfusion, each with specific availability and claim rules.
user_questions: Which outpatient benefit category is relevant?
Is the service available at the accredited facility?
Does the benefit have a session or day-limit rule?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain source-listed outpatient categories only. Do not calculate final benefits or advise where to undergo care.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: PhilHealth lists Z Benefits for selected high-cost conditions and procedures, with condition-specific benefit amounts and selection criteria.
user_questions: Is the condition or procedure listed under a Z Benefit package?
Which contracted facility handles the package?
What selection criteria and forms apply?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain that Z Benefits exist and require specific criteria. Do not determine patient eligibility or clinical suitability.

product_surfaces.product_name: MyMaxicare Individual and Family HMO Plans
product_surfaces.educational_summary: Maxicare presents MyMaxicare as full-risk, full-coverage HMO plans for individuals and families, while the IC circular supplies the broader HMO product taxonomy.
user_questions: Is the user shopping as an individual or family?
Which hospitals, clinics, and doctors must be verified in the network?
What exclusions, waiting periods, and annual benefit limits apply?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain the product surface and verification questions only. Do not recommend buying or claim that any doctor or hospital is covered.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: Maxicare lists programs for micro, small, and medium enterprises, including options intended for startups or growing businesses.
user_questions: How many employees and dependents are being enrolled?
Is the employer comparing outpatient-only and full HMO structures?
Who manages member enrollment, dependents, and plan changes?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain the employer plan surface only. Do not advise a business to select a specific plan.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: Maxicare lists corporate plans as comprehensive health benefits designed for employee needs.
user_questions: Is the user an employee trying to understand an employer-issued plan?
Where are the official HR, HMO, and policy documents?
What provider network and pre-approval workflow applies?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain that corporate HMO terms are employer and contract specific. Do not infer coverage from a generic provider page.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: Maxicare groups prepaid health cards separately from comprehensive plans, including PRIMA and emergency-oriented card products.
user_questions: Is the user looking at a prepaid card or a comprehensive HMO plan?
What exact card name and validity period apply?
Which services are limited to Maxicare clinics or listed partners?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Distinguish prepaid card surfaces from full HMO plans. Do not assume broad hospitalization coverage.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: Maxicare presents LifesavER products as emergency protection products, distinct from comprehensive HMO plan categories.
user_questions: Which LifesavER variant is being considered?
What emergency cases, network rules, and limits are listed in the product terms?
How does this differ from the user's existing PhilHealth or HMO coverage?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain product category distinctions only. Do not determine emergency eligibility or coverage.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: MediCard lists Standard as a package for hospitalization, outpatient, emergency-room, annual physical exam, and dental care services.
user_questions: Is the package for an individual or family?
What room, benefit, dental, and clinic limits appear in the plan terms?
Which services require LOA or prior coordination?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain provider-published categories only. Do not quote benefits as applicable without the current plan terms.

product_surfaces.product_name: MediCard HMO Plans for SMEs and Corporations
product_surfaces.educational_summary: MediCard lists HMO plans for SMEs and corporations as a separate package surface for employer-provided healthcare services.
user_questions: Is the user an employer, HR operator, or employee?
Where is the company-specific benefit schedule?
What enrollment, dependent, LOA, and provider-network steps apply?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain group HMO workflow questions only. Do not infer employer-specific benefits.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: MediCard presents H.E.R.O. as an emergency-response product surface, with its own annual coverage and emergency-use terms.
user_questions: Is the case trauma, non-trauma, or otherwise excluded?
What annual limit, waiting period, and renewal rule applies?
Which hospitals or emergency workflows accept the product?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain the emergency product surface only. Do not decide whether a specific emergency is covered.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: PhilCare describes Health LUXE as an all-inclusive health coverage program with preventive care, outpatient care, hospitalization, emergency care, travel assist, and lifestyle services.
user_questions: Is Health LUXE the exact product being evaluated?
Which hospitals, clinics, and assistance services are included in the current terms?
What conditions, limits, and exclusions are listed in the plan document?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain the provider-published product surface only. Do not recommend purchase or infer actual coverage.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: AIA Philippines presents health products as protection from the financial impact of health-related expenses and serious illnesses.
user_questions: Is the product a medical plan, critical illness plan, rider, or life insurance policy with health benefits?
What cash benefit, reimbursement, premium, waiting period, and exclusions apply?
Which licensed advisor or official policy document should verify details?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain insurer health-protection category only. Do not recommend a policy or provide financial advice.

product_surfaces.educational_summary: Sun Life lists multiple health protection products, including plans with critical illness, life insurance, hospitalization, wellness, senior, and OFW-oriented surfaces.
user_questions: Which named Sun Life product is being compared?
Is the benefit a cash benefit, reimbursement, life insurance benefit, or rider?
What advisor-provided illustration and policy contract governs the benefit?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain product families only. Do not rank products or provide investment, financial, or insurance advice.

product_aliases: Pru Life UK critical illness protection
product_surfaces.educational_summary: Pru Life UK presents health products as helping ease the financial burden from hospitalization or critical illness, including diagnosis claims, daily hospital income, and critical illness coverage categories.
user_questions: Which named Pru Life UK health product applies?
Is the user looking for hospitalization support, critical illness protection, or another policy structure?
What exclusions, waiting periods, and policy contract terms govern the product?
product_surfaces.advice_boundary: Explain the insurer product category only. Do not recommend a policy or provide financial advice.
